ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Levin and Pamela Louie for Martha Maierhauser, James Kavanaugh and Marilyn Kavanaugh (Co-Executors).

Counsel addressed the issues raised by the Court with respect to the Trust amendments allowing splitting of the corpus of the Trust when the first Beneficiary reaches thirty, the suggestion of naming two Trustees when the will is very clear as to one, and allowing fees and expenses on an hourly basis/rate. Ms. Levin is to provide a revised order with a named single Trustee and with the full will language for fees. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Amend Order For Probate
Off Calendar Nov-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Amend Order For Probate
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Continued To Nov-17-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Louie for Martha Maierhauser.

Ms. Louie stated that it may be overwhelming to have one person sign all of the checks, that it is a complicated estate and that there is an apartment complex that is owned by the estate which requires dealing with bills and rents. The Petition to Amend Order for Probate was continued to November 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for either an amended petition to nominate one person to act or perhaps some other solution.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Naznin Challa for Martha Maierhauser, James Kavanaugh and Marilyn Kavanaugh.

Ms. Challa stated that the Decedent drafted her own Wills and used incorrect language, that the 2012 Will disposes 100% of the residue of the Estate and the personal property, and that the 2005 Will was done by an Attorney and from then on the Decedent did the Wills herself. Ms. Challa also stated that the Decedent was in and out of the hospital, that she could have taken the original will with her to the hospital, that 10 or 12 other people had keys to the Decedent's house and that she does not know if the Will got disposed of accidently. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
